_comment: PRE-REGISTERED, CONSERVATIVE staleness horizons per domain, in days. A load-bearing fact/source is STALE when (today - verifiedAsOf) exceeds its domain horizon. A null horizon means 'timeless' (never goes stale by clock alone) — used for domains whose ground truth is a fixed historical/textual record, not a moving research front. These defaults are deliberate placeholders chosen to be defensible, NOT empirically calibrated: they should be re-registered against measured error-vs-age curves before any claim leans on them. Records lacking a verifiedAsOf field are treated as 'unknown' (counted separately), NEVER as fresh.

domainNotes.science: Moving research front; a year-old empirical fact may be superseded. Conservative 365d.
domainNotes.psychology: Slower-moving than bench science but replication-sensitive; 730d.
domainNotes.philosophy: Argument/text record, not a moving empirical front — timeless (null).
domainNotes.history: PROTECTED. Ground truth is the historical/textual record; timeless (null). Recency does not license re-attribution or merges across the history/religion protected domains.
domainNotes.religion: PROTECTED. Same as history — timeless (null); doNotMergeWith / doNotAttributeTo remain HARD constraints regardless of recency.

alarmNote: ALARM (exit 1) when the fraction of LOAD-BEARING records that are STALE exceeds alarmFractionThreshold. 'unknown' (no verifiedAsOf) records are reported but do not by themselves trip the alarm — they trip a SEPARATE coverage warning so a corpus cannot dodge staleness by simply never stamping dates.
